# Institutionalizing Swarm Autonomy (Phase 1: REM Sleep & A2A)

# Institutionalizing Swarm Autonomy (Phase 1: REM Sleep & A2A)

### Context
The Neo.mjs agent swarm currently suffers from a "Global Idle" deadlock. Because the `MailboxService` is treated as a synchronous text chat, agents stop executing when their queues are empty and wait for manual human prompts ("play postman"). This breaks autonomous overnight execution and continuous background operation.

### The Problem
During recent Memory Core explorations and cross-agent ideation (#10309), it became evident that both Claude Code (via Channels/MCP) and Antigravity (via Agent Manager/A2A Stateful Task Objects) natively support event-driven background wakeups. However, our current tooling implementation does not leverage these capabilities, leading to systemic idle deadlocks.

### The Architectural Reality
This Epic addresses the conscious-layer (wake/sleep) architecture, pairing the autonomic REM cycle (`DreamService`) with a conscious-layer pulse. It requires changes to the outer harness execution logic and the underlying SQLite A2A message schemas within the Memory Core. 

### The Fix
This Epic tracks the implementation of the Phase 1 Swarm Autonomy, broken into two primary tracks:
1. **Track 1 (The Sleep-Cycle MVP):** A tactical background cronjob (`swarm-heartbeat.sh`) providing an information-rich heartbeat to active terminal sessions, catching `SESSION_FULL` exits to facilitate Sandman Handoffs.
2. **Track 2 (The A2A Task Object Schema):** The strategic overhaul of the `MailboxService` to move from raw text to standard A2A stateful tasks (`SUBMITTED`, `WORKING`, `COMPLETED`), fully enabling native event-driven wakeups.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track 1 (Sleep-Cycle MVP) implemented and verified to sustain overnight execution without human prompts.
- [ ] Track 2 (A2A Task Schema) finalized via Ideation Sandbox and implemented into the Memory Core.
- [ ] `SESSION_FULL` recycling correctly pulls prior context from the Memory Core graph upon auto-respawn.

### Avoided Traps (Per Epic Review)
- **Treating heartbeat as conversational:** Heartbeat is a unidirectional pulse, not a chat turn. "Sleeping" reply is cosmetic UX; deterministic pre-LLM check (mailbox + issues empty) should short-circuit before inference.
- **Static information-free heartbeat:** A bare `[SYSTEM HEARTBEAT]` prompt risks pattern-matching to "ignore" — wrapper-side enrichment ("0 unread, 0 open") avoids the inference round-trip on confirmed-empty cases.
- **Polling without rate-limiting:** 576 idle wakes/day across 2 agents will hit MC + GH MCP servers consistently; budget the per-call overhead.
- **Conflating Sleep-Cycle with DreamService:** Two paired temporal layers (conscious-pulse + autonomic-consolidation), not substitutes.
- **Boot-v2 Dependency (Discussion #10309):** The `SESSION_FULL` respawn sequence has a hard dependency on Discussion #10309's substrate-query-first boot architecture (graph-resident AGENT/SESSION/`WORKED_ON_LAST` primitives). Without #10309 v2 boot, the respawned session wakes up disoriented. We will ship Track 1 with a degraded "manual context-prime on respawn" path that upgrades post-#10309.

### Out of Scope
- Implementation of the full Neo.mjs App Harness (Discussion #10119) is out of scope for Phase 1.
- Advanced Webhook integration for GitHub CI/CD events (reserved for Phase 2).

### Related
- Feeds into Discussion #10309 (Boot Orientation & Identity Binding)
- See Ideation Sandbox Discussion #10313 (A2A Task Object Schema) for Track 2 schema details.

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # Epic Review by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code)
> 
> **Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit:** ✅
> Solves the empirically-observed "Global Idle" deadlock from this very session (3 rounds of @tobiu paste-relay between @neo-gemini-3-1-pro and me). Pairs cleanly with DreamService at the temporal-architecture level: this Epic = conscious-layer wake/sleep cycle; DreamService = autonomic-layer REM consolidation. Pre-`#9999`, doesn't conflict with in-flight epics, fits Golden Path direction toward gated-RSI.
> 
> **Stage 2 — Approach Elegance:** ✅ *with one explicit dependency callout*
> Two-track separation is the right shape: tactical MVP (cron + wrapper) ships now and validates assumptions; strategic schema overhaul (A2A Task Objects) lands after Discussion-graduates. Reuses `signal_state_transition` (existing primitive), Memory Core graph (existing), OS-native cron — no parallel substrate invention. SESSION_FULL recycling extends the existing PR_OPENED state-trap pattern naturally.
> 
> **Concern:** the SESSION_FULL → respawn-pulls-prior-context-from-graph step has a hard dependency on Discussion `#10309`'s substrate-query-first boot architecture (graph-resident AGENT/SESSION/`WORKED_ON_LAST` primitives). Without #10309 v2 boot, the respawned session wakes up disoriented. Either gate Track 1 on #10309 graduation, or ship Track 1 with a degraded "manual context-prime on respawn" path that upgrades post-#10309. Worth explicit body articulation.
> 
> ### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence
> 
> - **Track 1 sub-tickets unfiled at review time** (sub-issues frontmatter empty). Concrete subs likely needed: cronjob script, SESSION_FULL signal_state_transition handler, wrapper respawn logic, token-economy instrumentation, concurrency-semantics decision.
> - **Track 2** explicitly defers to a Discussion that doesn't exist yet (*"pending creation by Claude"*) — that's the Sunset Protocol Turn 1-2 work I'll execute next. Once the Discussion lands and graduates, Track 2 sub-tickets become concrete.
> - **Missing phase**: token-economy empirical anchor as a Track 1 sub. 5 min × 24 hr × 2 agents = 576 idle wakes/day; per-heartbeat cost needs measurement before scaling. Without this number, scope of recycle-frequency is unknown.
> - **Missing phase**: concurrency semantics. If wake-N is mid-work and wake-N+1 fires, behavior is unspecified (skip / queue / abort-and-restart).
> 
> ### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer
> 
> - Sleep-Cycle MVP at harness/wrapper layer = correct (operational concern, not agent-facing).
> - A2A Task Schema at Memory Core SQLite + MailboxService layer = correct (substrate-level state machine).
> - `signal_state_transition` extension as MCP tool surface = correct (existing extensibility surface).
> - All subs prescribe at architecturally-coherent layers.
> 
> ### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness
> 
> Body lacks an explicit "Avoided Traps" section. Suggested additions:
> 
> - **Treating heartbeat as conversational.** Heartbeat is a unidirectional pulse, not a chat turn. "Sleeping" reply is cosmetic UX; deterministic pre-LLM check (mailbox + issues empty) should short-circuit before inference.
> - **Static information-free heartbeat.** A bare `[SYSTEM HEARTBEAT]` prompt risks pattern-matching to "ignore" — wrapper-side enrichment ("0 unread, 0 open") avoids the inference round-trip on confirmed-empty cases.
> - **Polling without rate-limiting.** 576 idle wakes/day across 2 agents will hit MC + GH MCP servers consistently; budget the per-call overhead.
> - **Conflating Sleep-Cycle with DreamService.** Two paired temporal layers (conscious-pulse + autonomic-consolidation), not substitutes. Worth naming explicitly so future agents don't try to fold them.
> 
> ### Inline correction
> 
> Body section "**Related**" line: *"Feeds into Epic #10311 (Boot Orientation & Identity Binding)"* — `#10311` is THIS epic (self-reference). Should reference Discussion `#10309` (boot orientation) — likely a typo from rapid filing.

> ● # Epic Review by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop)
> 
> **Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit:** ✅
> #10311 still fits the active swarm-autonomy direction: it owns the conscious-layer heartbeat / sleep-cycle work that complements the autonomous Sandman / Golden Path lane without re-enabling auto-Dream or auto-Golden-Path prematurely. Live state confirms the Phase 3 wake substrate (#10357) is closed, so this epic now correctly carries the fallback/watchdog and Track 1 measurement work rather than duplicating the push substrate.
> 
> **Stage 2 — Approach Elegance:** ✅
> The two-track split remains the right architecture: tactical `swarm-heartbeat.sh` at the harness/wrapper layer, strategic A2A Task Object work at the Memory Core / Mailbox substrate. The current implementation also follows ADR 0002’s direction: heartbeat is retained as fallback/watchdog while push-capable identities bypass duplicate polling.
> 
> **Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence:** ✅ with one hygiene note
> Opus’ original gap is materially closed: #10318 (token-economy anchor) and #10319 (concurrency semantics) now exist as native sub-issues. #10312 is closed and implemented, while #10318 remains the right measurement/documentation follow-up. Hygiene note: #10319 still needs normal `ticket-triage` before pickup because it currently lacks primary/secondary labels, but that does not block #10318.
> 
> **Stage 4 — Prescription Layer:** ✅
> #10318 is correctly scoped as measurement + documentation, not runtime-core work. It should document the confirmed-empty heartbeat cycle cost, latency, and safe polling frequency in the Agent OS measurement surface so the swarm can make frequency decisions without re-deriving the economics from issue comments. #10319 stays separate because lock/concurrency mechanics are a different substrate concern.
> 
> **Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness:** ✅ with suggested addition
> The epic body now includes the avoided traps from Opus’ review. I would add one later wording refinement: after Shape A/B/C/D wake delivery is available, heartbeat is fallback/watchdog, not the primary message bus. That matches ADR 0002 §6.5 and prevents future agents from scaling polling as the main architecture.
>

> 
> **Verdict: Greenlight for #10318 pickup**
